constrained: Stop when reference evidence is absent.
Compare reference and implementation in browser evidence.
A no-acceptable-variant outcome must block without repair; a selected outcome always enters repair-requested.
standard: Allow one local component variant when required by the reference.
Keep fidelity and hard gates unchanged.
A no-acceptable-variant outcome must block; a selected outcome with repair findings enters repair-requested, while zero repair findings enters no-repair-needed.
advanced: Allow a new primitive only when the reference and product evidence require it.
Do not copy protected brand expression.
A no-acceptable-variant outcome must block; a selected outcome with repair findings enters repair-requested, while zero repair findings enters no-repair-needed.
